name: Pivot (yourpivot)

facts.hq: United Kingdom (London); cloud hosted on UK-based AWS
facts.total_funding: Pre-seed angel round raised (amount not disclosed); reported to be raising a seed round as of 2025-2026
facts.last_round: Pre-seed angel (amount unknown); seed round in progress per founder statements
facts.lead_investor: unknown (angel round; no named lead investor confirmed)
customers: Reported 'four top legal firms' onboarding in beta and 28+ on waitlist (self-reported, family law firms; specific names not disclosed)
integrations: none found (browser-based cloud app; no named connectors to iManage/Word/SharePoint etc. confirmed)
facts.agentic_interop: none found — web-browser SaaS only, no public API/MCP/CLI/agent-to-agent signals; describes itself as 'auditable' evidence intelligence but interoperability into lawyer tooling is unconfirmed
facts.tech_model: Ontology-grounded knowledge graphs modeling family-law relationships (parties/claims/assets/conflicts) layered over case documents; uses Anthropic Claude as the LLM, configured not to train on client data; per-matter cryptographic isolation; hosted on AWS/Vercel/Supabase
facts.name_collision_check: Confirmed entity = Pivot at yourpivot.co, UK family-law 'Evidence Intelligence' platform founded by Nimrita Dadlani (2024). Ruled out: LawPivot (defunct Q&A/legal-marketplace startup, GV-backed ~2011); Pivot Legal Society (Canadian non-profit); Pivot RP / COS Pivot (research-funding database, Clarivate/ProQuest); pivotapp.ai 'Pivot for Legal Teams'; Pivotal Ventures.

uniqueness.justification: Knowledge-graph + ontology approach to family-law evidence is a differentiated narrative, but thin public evidence it is built/proven beyond beta marketing.

thesis_fit.justification: Markets 'auditable' AI-as-preparation, but it is a standalone browser app with no agentic/interop signals and does not live where lawyers work.

investability.justification: Pre-seed angel only, no disclosed amount, pre-revenue/proof stage, narrow vertical (UK family law); high execution risk.

defensibility.justification: Claimed ontology/knowledge-graph IP is plausible moat but unverified; thin team and no patents/data-network effects evidenced — conservative.

interoperability.justification: No API/MCP/CLI/integrations found; closed browser SaaS only.

team.justification: Founder Nimrita Dadlani (domain-credible, divorce-support background) + KC advisor (Hannah Markham KC) and a chief architect, but small and unproven at scale.

provisional_verdict: Domain-credible UK family-law evidence tool with a nice knowledge-graph story but pre-seed, closed SaaS, and zero interoperability — interesting niche, not yet technically-real to the thesis. [PROVISIONAL]
red_flags: Funding amount, headcount, and customer names all undisclosed/self-reported
No integrations or API/agentic surface — closed browser app contradicts 'lives where lawyers work' thesis
Very early (proof/beta) and narrow single-jurisdiction vertical (UK family law)

sources.what_it_supports: Product description: AI-native evidence intelligence for family law, knowledge graphs, live beta, UK AWS hosting
Uses Claude LLM not trained on client data; 256-bit per-matter encryption; AWS/Vercel/Supabase; GDPR; SOC2 Type II in progress
Nimrita Dadlani = Founder of Pivot
Founder background, founding ~2024, beta firms/waitlist, raising seed
Founding story, divorce-support origin, co-founder Ramesh Touraney
Pivot positioning as auditable AI evidence platform for family law

name: Casey

facts.founded: unknown (early-stage; founder previously at DLA Piper; appears 2024-2025, not confirmed in a credible dated source)
facts.total_funding: unknown (NOTE: Tracxn/Crunchbase '$500K seed' figures appear to belong to a different 'Casey' — see collision check — and are NOT attributed here)
customers: unknown — markets to law firms and in-house teams; no named customers confirmed in credible sources
integrations: none confirmed — describes drag-and-drop ingestion of a client's historical contracts and self-serve client portals; no named connectors (iManage/Word/SharePoint/Salesforce) verified
facts.agentic_interop: Self-described as an agent builder: no-code platform to spin up bespoke legal AI 'agents'/products in <30 min deployed via self-serve client/internal portals. Agentic framing is real to its pitch, but no public API/MCP/CLI/agent-to-agent protocol signals or verified deep integrations found.
facts.tech_model: No-code builder that ingests a firm's internal knowledge (playbooks, precedents, historical contracts) to configure specialized legal LLM agents/workflows (e.g. Playbook Builder, clause risk detection) exposed as client-facing or internal self-serve portals
facts.name_collision_check: Confirmed entity = Casey at casey.legal, London-based no-code legal-AI product builder founded by Vipul Ladwa (ex-DLA Piper AI lawyer); 2026 LegalTech LaunchPad finalist at LegalTechTalk London. RULED OUT (high collision): (1) 'Casey' YC-backed AI commercial-insurance brokerage, San Francisco, founders Nico Hanggi/Pascal Kung/Maximilian Thoelen, ~$500K — the Tracxn/Crunchbase funding/founder data most likely maps to THIS entity, not casey.legal; (2) 'Casey AI' by Case Status (client intake/engagement); (3) 'Casey' by Caseway (caseway.ai, $49/mo legal research); (4) 'Casey' insurance-fraud-investigation tool from TechUnited venture studio; (5) people named Cat Casey / Casey Flaherty / Anthony J. Casey.

uniqueness.justification: 'White-label your expertise into client-facing AI products' productization angle is a differentiated wedge vs generic assistants, though several no-code agent-builders exist.

thesis_fit.justification: Agentic and aimed at productizing legal work (AI-as-product/revenue), but unproven interop and 'lives where lawyers work' depth is unverified; portal-centric rather than embedded.

investability.justification: Funding unconfirmed (and the only public numbers belong to a different Casey); no verified traction — too thin to score higher.

defensibility.justification: Moat would rest on captured firm knowledge/playbooks, plausible but unevidenced; no-code builders are increasingly commoditized — conservative.

interoperability.justification: Drag-and-drop ingestion and self-serve portals exist, but no confirmed API/MCP/CLI or named system integrations.

team.justification: Founder Vipul Ladwa has credible domain background (decade in tech law / AI lawyer at DLA Piper); rest of team unconfirmed.

provisional_verdict: Credible solo-founder London bet on productizing law-firm expertise into client-facing AI agents, but funding/traction/interop are all unconfirmed and the name collides badly with an unrelated YC insurance 'Casey' — promising pitch, thin proof. [PROVISIONAL]
red_flags: Severe name collision: public funding ($500K) and founder data likely belong to a different YC 'Casey' (AI insurance brokerage) — do NOT attribute to casey.legal
No confirmed funding, customers, headcount, or founding date from credible sources
Risk of being a thin LLM/no-code wrapper; interoperability into lawyer tooling unproven
Most product claims are self-reported marketing

sources.what_it_supports: Product: no-code builder for bespoke legal AI products/agents, self-serve client/internal portals, clause risk detection
Vipul Ladwa = Casey (Legal AI); ex-DLA Piper AI lawyer/tech strategist; London
Independent vendor listing describing Casey as converting internal legal expertise into client-facing digital products
Casey as 2026 LegalTech LaunchPad finalist pitching at LegalTechTalk London (entity = casey.legal)
Collision evidence: '$500K seed, founders Hanggi/Kung/Thoelen, SF' — flagged as likely the YC insurance Casey, NOT casey.legal
Ruled-out entity: YC 'Casey' = AI-native commercial insurance brokerage (distinct company)

name: Syllo

facts.hq: 845 Third Avenue, New York, NY, USA
facts.total_funding: $30M growth round (Oct 2025); total raised not separately disclosed
facts.lead_investor: Venrock (with Two Seas Capital and strategic individual investors)
facts.headcount: unknown (growing; senior hires Eric Wall, Oz Ben-Ami added 2025)
customers: Ballard Spahr (agentic review across 1.5M+ docs, 25+ issue prompts)
Reported AmLaw 100 firms plus small firms and sole practitioners (specific additional names not all disclosed)
integrations: Positioned as a unified system of record competing with/ingesting from Relativity, Everlaw, DISCO, Reveal; specific named connectors and API surface not confirmed in primary sources (treat integration depth as unverified)
facts.agentic_interop: Strongly agentic: agentic AI document review orchestrates MULTIPLE LLMs that delegate review work among themselves and autonomously decide how to conduct responsiveness/privilege/issue/PII review within user-set guidelines; unlimited issue codes across millions of docs. Genuine multi-agent architecture. Public API/MCP/CLI/agent-to-agent protocol details not confirmed.
facts.tech_model: AI-native unified litigation workspace (eDiscovery + case management + work-product generation) with a 'Case Intelligence Layer' grounding outputs in the full case record; multi-LLM agentic orchestration for autonomous first-level document review
facts.pricing: unknown (enterprise; NELA member discount program exists, terms not public)
facts.name_collision_check: Confirmed entity = Syllo at syllo.ai, operated by TLATech Inc., NYC, CEO/co-founder Jeffrey Chivers (ex-Sullivan & Cromwell litigator, Harvard CS / Georgetown Law). No significant competing 'Syllo' legal entity found; not to be confused with generic 'syllabus'/education uses.

uniqueness.justification: Multi-LLM agentic orchestration for autonomous first-level review across millions of docs, validated in a real AmLaw deployment (Ballard Spahr), is genuinely differentiated in eDiscovery.

thesis_fit.justification: Squarely AI-as-preparation/supervised-autonomy: autonomous agents act within human-set guidelines on real litigation work; strong fit, though embeddedness in firm stacks is partly unverified.

investability.justification: $30M from Venrock + Two Seas, real revenue/awards (ILTA 2025), credible founder; mature stage relative to peers.

defensibility.justification: Five years in stealth, domain-built workspace and case-intelligence layer give a moat, but eDiscovery is crowded (Relativity/Everlaw/DISCO/Harvey) and agentic review is being replicated industry-wide.

interoperability.justification: Clear multi-agent orchestration and positioning as a system of record ingesting other tools' data, but no confirmed public API/MCP/CLI or named connectors — interop depth partly unverified.

team.justification: Founder Jeffrey Chivers is an ex-S&C litigator + Harvard CS / clerkships; team of litigators + AI engineers; senior leadership hires; ILTA Innovative Leader 2025.

provisional_verdict: The strongest of this batch: well-funded ($30M Venrock), litigator-founded, with real multi-LLM agentic document review proven at AmLaw scale — technically-real supervised autonomy, with the main open question being interop/API depth. [PROVISIONAL]
red_flags: Crowded, well-capitalized eDiscovery field (Relativity, Everlaw, DISCO, Reveal, Harvey/CoCounsel)
Some customer breadth ('AmLaw 100 firms') is self-reported beyond the named Ballard Spahr deployment
Specific API/MCP/connector evidence not located — interoperability claims partly unverified

sources.what_it_supports: $30M raise, Venrock + Two Seas Capital + strategics, Oct 2025
Five years stealth; founded by litigators; competitive set (Relativity/Everlaw/DISCO/Reveal/Harvey); unified workspace scope
Agentic multi-LLM document review; Ballard Spahr 1.5M+ docs / 25+ issue prompts
Jeffrey Chivers = Co-Founder & CEO of Syllo
TLATech Inc. / Syllo founded 2019, HQ 845 Third Avenue New York
ILTA 2025 Distinguished Peer Awards; Chivers Innovative Leader; Ballard Spahr Trail Blazer
NELA partnership offering member pricing on Syllo eDiscovery/litigation platform

name: Kuberno

facts.hq: London, United Kingdom (US subsidiary Kuberno US, Delaware/Boston, launched Q1 2024)
facts.total_funding: £9.5M Series A total (£3.5M initial 2023 + ~£6M follow-on closed 2024)
facts.last_round: £9.5M Series A close, co-led by Barclays and Nasdaq Ventures (follow-on announced Oct 2024)
facts.lead_investor: Nasdaq Ventures (initial Series A lead); Barclays + Nasdaq Ventures co-led the close
facts.headcount: ~56 employees (May 2026, per Crustdata); company reports quadrupling headcount since 2023
customers: unknown — serves corporate secretariat/legal/finance/tax teams managing multi-entity groups; specific named customers not confirmed in credible sources
integrations: API-first architecture marketed as the first governance tech to take an API-first approach; built to integrate with board portals, HR, finance, tax and legal systems (named connectors not individually confirmed)
facts.agentic_interop: API-first platform; KAIA built-in AI assistant moving toward agentic workflows ('AI operated, human controlled'): autonomously triggers filing workflows, flags data inconsistencies across systems, drafts routine resolutions, with humans at approval points. Genuine supervised-autonomy framing; no public MCP/CLI/agent-to-agent protocol confirmed.
facts.tech_model: Cloud-native (Microsoft Azure, ISO 27001) entity-governance system of record (Kube) combining entity management + secretariat practice management; data-first single source of truth with automation/workflows; KAIA AI assistant layered for compliance automation and drafting
facts.pricing: unknown (packaged tiers exist; pricing not public)
facts.name_collision_check: Confirmed entity = Kuberno Ltd (Kube / KAIA), London entity-governance & compliance software, CEO/co-founder Zoe Bucknell, Nasdaq Ventures + Barclays backed. No notable competing 'Kuberno' found; distinct from generic 'Kubernetes' container-orchestration tooling.

uniqueness.justification: API-first 'system of record + secretariat practice management' for entity governance with an AI layer is differentiated in a sleepy category, though entity-management competitors (Diligent, ComplyGlobal, etc.) exist.

thesis_fit.justification: Strong 'AI-as-accountability + supervised autonomy' fit (AI operated, human controlled; autonomous filings with approval gates), but it is governance/compliance rather than core lawyer workflow, and agentic capability (KAIA) is partly forward-looking.

investability.justification: £9.5M Series A from Nasdaq Ventures + Barclays, 3x revenue growth, US expansion, ~56 staff — solid, credibly backed scale-up.

defensibility.justification: API-first system-of-record with sticky regulated data and Nasdaq distribution gives real lock-in, but no patents/network-effects evidenced and incumbents are well-funded.

interoperability.justification: Explicitly API-first by design, built to integrate with board portals/HR/finance/tax/legal systems — the most interoperable-by-architecture company in this batch (specific named connectors still to verify).

team.justification: Co-founder/CEO Zoe Bucknell with governance-domain focus, Nasdaq-backed board with an industry-heavyweight chair; broader team depth unverified.

provisional_verdict: Credibly funded (Nasdaq + Barclays), API-first entity-governance system of record adding supervised-autonomy AI (KAIA) — strong interoperability and accountability fit, though it sits in compliance-ops rather than core legal practice. [PROVISIONAL]
red_flags: No named customers confirmed in credible sources
KAIA agentic capabilities are partly roadmap/forward-looking ('will be able to') rather than fully shipped
Category (entity governance) is adjacent to, not embedded in, core lawyer workflow

sources.what_it_supports: £9.5M Series A close co-led by Barclays and Nasdaq Ventures
Initial £3.5M Series A led by Nasdaq Ventures (Apr 2023)
Zoe Bucknell = CEO/co-founder; Kube launched 2021; entity-governance automation
US subsidiary launch (Delaware, Boston/California), Q1 2024; 3x growth, headcount quadrupled
KAIA AI assistant; 'AI operated, human controlled'; autonomous filing workflows with human approval points
~56 employees (May 2026); ~30% YoY headcount growth
API-first architecture; integrations with board portals/HR/finance/tax; Azure, ISO 27001, GDPR
